<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kengoshizuka Kofun]][[en:Kengoshizuka Kofun]][[ja:牽牛子塚古墳]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kengoshizuka Kofun]][[en:Kengoshizuka Kofun]][[ja:牽牛子塚古墳]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kengoshizuka Kofun]][[ja:牽牛子塚古墳]]









{{Short description|Tomb of Empress Kōgyoku of Japan}}{{Expand Japanese}}{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
|name=Kengoshizuka Kofun
|image=File:220311 Kengoshiduka 1.jpg
|imagecaption=Full view of the mound (2022)
|location={{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397|1=Asuka, Nara}}
|coordinates={{Coord|34.466306|N|135.792303|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
|map_size=Main mound diameter approx. 22m (including paved stone and gravel areas, 32m)
|built=Mid {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} to early {{ill|8th century|en|8th century|qid=Q8086|lt=8th century|1=8th century}}
|Occupant=(Hypothetical) {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} (37th Emperor)
|burial goods=Lacquered coffin fragments, {{ill|Cloisonné|en|Cloisonné|qid=Q1102226|lt=Cloisonné|1=Cloisonné}} decorative fittings, molars
|Historic Site=National Historic Site: "Kengoshizuka Kofun and Kosotsukamikado Kofun"
|TangibleHistoric Site=burial goods Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}
|map={{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=27|coordinates=58.7|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=32.29|map_dot_label=Kengoshizuka<br>Mausoleum|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}}
|architectural_styles={{ill|octagonal kofun|qid=Q11391751|1=Octagonal Kofun}}
| Interred_individual = {{ill|Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}
}}

'''Kengoshizuka Kofun''' is a [[Kofun]] in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}} Japan.<ref>Tokuse, Takahiko, and Eriko Oka. "Life in Archaeological Sites and Settlements in Asuka Village as a Model Case for Managing Settlements Adjacent to Archaeological Sites: A Proposal for the Future of Sa11ara." The journal of Center for the Global Study of Historic Site and Culture 2 (2015): 5-20.</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Kofuns in Europe and Japan: Comparative and Contextual Perspectives |date=30 September 2018 |doi=10.2307/j.ctv1228gf7|s2cid=240193791 }}</ref> Specifically, it is an [[Octagonal Kofun]]. It is said to be the burial site of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Kōgyoku|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Kengoshizuka Kofun Kofun, Koshitsuka-gomon Kofun Kofun |url=http://asuka-japan-heritage.jp/global/en/saimei/detail/kengosiduka_sa.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=Exploring the Footsteps of the Heroines of Asuka |language=en}}</ref><ref name="Mainichi Daily News-2022">{{Cite news |date=2022-03-04 |title=Octagonal Kofun said to belong to ancient Japan empress reconstructed in Nara Pref. |language=en |work=Mainichi Daily News |url=https://mainichi.jp/english/articles/20220304/p2a/00m/0na/040000c |access-date=2023-10-20}}</ref> it was reconstructed and opened to the public on May 6 2022.<ref name="Mainichi Daily News-2022" /> This contrasts with other Imperial tombs such as the {{ill|Gobyō-no-Kofun|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} which are closed to the public by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Gobyōyama Kofun (in Kyōto) - Passage Grave in Japan in Honshū |url=https://www.megalithic.co.uk/article.php?sid=33021 |access-date=2023-10-21 |website=The Megalithic Portal}}</ref>
[[File:220311 Kengoshiduka 3.jpg|thumb|Entrance to the burial chamber]]

==overview==

'''Kengoshizuka Kofun''' (牽牛子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site. The excavated burial goods from the site are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.

The name "Kengoshizuka" includes the word "{{ill|Ipomoea nil|en|Ipomoea nil|qid=Q162117|lt=Asagao|1=Ipomoea nil}}" ({{ill|Ipomoea_nil|en|Ipomoea_nil|qid=Q162117|lt=Morning Glory|1=Ipomoea nil}}), an alternate reading for "Kengoshi." The tumulus is also referred to as "Omae-zuka."<ref name="asuka">[http://www.asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/kengoshizuka/kengoshizuka.html Kengoshizuka Kofun] Asuka Village Official Website</ref> Excavations conducted between 2009 and 2010 revealed that the tumulus was octagonal, strengthening its association with {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}, mother of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} and {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}. Full restoration was completed in February 2022.<ref>Nara's Asuka Morning Glory Kofun fully restored, "Mainichi Shimbun," March 3, 2022, Osaka Morning Edition</ref>

== location ==

The tumulus is located near the border of {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara|1=Kashihara, Nara}} and lies at the stable, broad apex of a hill in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}. Its summit reaches an elevation of 126.3 meters and is approximately 500 meters west of the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|en|Iwayayama Kofun|qid=Q11474389|lt=Iwayayama Kofun|1=Iwayayama Kofun}}, another National Historic Site.

== Exterior and map_size ==

The site was previously known for its unique internal structure, consisting of two hollowed chambers within a massive stone. This led to theories that it served as a combined tomb for Empress Saimei and her daughter {{ill|Princess Hashihito|en|Princess Hashihito|qid=Q11655373|lt=Princess Hashihito|1=Princess Hashihito}} (consort of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|qid=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}). Excavations in 1977–1978 created measured diagrams based on surveys, and while it was initially reported as a circular tumulus,<ref name="jiten">Ōtsuka, Hatsushige, and Kobayashi, Saburō (1982). *Dictionary of Kofun.* Tokyo: Tōkyodō Publishing, pp. 108-109.</ref> the possibility of an octagonal structure was also noted.

From September 2009 to September 2010, academic excavations by the Asuka Village Board of Education confirmed the octagonal layout.<ref name="mai1">[http://mainichi.jp/select/wadai/news/20100910k0000m040050000c.html Kengoshizuka Kofun: Confirmed as Octagonal burial and Likely burial Site of Empress Saimei.] "Mainichi Shimbun," September 9, 2010.</ref><ref name="mai2">[http://mainichi.jp/select/wadai/news/20100910k0000m040072000c.html Kengoshizuka Kofun: A 550-ton Stone Mausoleum Exhibiting Authority.] "Mainichi Shimbun," September 9, 2010.</ref><ref name="jiji">[http://www.jiji.com/jc/c?g=soc_30&k=2010090900686 Octagonal Mound Confirmed for Kengoshizuka Kofun: Further Evidence of Empress Saimei's Tomb.] "Jiji Press," September 9, 2010.</ref>

The mound was found to be a triple-tiered octagonal tumulus standing approximately 4.5 meters tall, with the base measuring 22 meters across. Surrounding the mound is a paved stone structure with a similar octagonal type, extending the total diameter to approximately 32 meters, including gravel layers.

Hundreds of meticulously carved white tuff stones were uncovered, estimated to have originally numbered 7,200 and used to create a pyramid-like exterior.<ref>Asahi Shimbun, September 10, 2010.</ref>

== Interior burial ==

The interior is a {{ill|yokoguchi-style sekkaku|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style sekkaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (side-entry stone chamber), partially exposed as of 2009. The chamber is carved from a massive block of tuff weighing approximately 80 tons, sourced from {{ill|Mount_Nijō|en|Mount_Nijō|qid=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} 15 kilometers away. The area of the chamber are 5 meters wide, 3.5 meters deep, and 2.5 meters high, divided into two spaces by a central partition wall.<ref name="jiten" /> 

The total weight of the stones used in the construction exceeds 550 tons. Transporting such massive stones over long distances required significant manpower, highlighting the power of the centralized state.<ref name="mai2" />

== burial goods ==

burial goods include lacquered coffin fragments, gold-copper fittings with {{ill|Cloisonné|en|Cloisonné|qid=Q1102226|lt=Cloisonné|1=Cloisonné}} patterns, glass beads, and human molars believed to belong to Princess Hashihito. These were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1953.<ref>{{国cultural property等データベース|201|9725|牽牛子塚古墳Excavated Artifacts}}</ref>

== Cultural Significance ==

The Kofun's unique octagonal type aligns it with other imperial tombs such as the {{ill|Dannotsuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (tomb of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}) and the {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Ouka Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Ouka Tomb|qq=}} (joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}). This reinforces its probable identification as the tomb of Empress Saimei.<ref name="mai2" />

== Adjacent Kofun ==

In December 2010, the Asuka Village Board of Education announced the discovery of a smaller kofun adjacent to Kengoshizuka Kofun. This nearby tomb, named {{ill|Kosotsukamikado Kofun|ja|越塚御門古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosotsukamikado Kofun|qq=}}, is hypothesized to be the tomb of Princess Ōta, Empress Saimei's gran11aughter.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20101211190601/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/culture/news/20101209-OYT1T00770.htm Adjacent Tomb Likely Princess Ōta's burial Site.] Yomiuri Shimbun, December 9, 2010.</ref>

== Access ==

* Approximately 550 meters northwest of {{ill|Asuka Station|en|Asuka Station|qid=Q4811860|lt=Asuka Station|1=Asuka Station}} ({{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|qid=Q901085|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line|1=Yoshino Line}}).

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== External Links ==
{{Commonscat|Kengoshizuka Kofun}}
* [http://www.asukamura.jp/chosa_hokoku/kengoshizuka/kengoshizuka.html Kengoshizuka Kofun] - Asuka Village Official Website
* {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1945|牽牛子塚古墳・越塚御門古墳}}
* {{国cultural property等データベース|201|9725|Yamato Province 高市郡牽牛子塚古墳Excavated Artifacts}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

Kengoshizuka Kofun
{{Archaeology-stub}}
{{Japan-stub}}

{{Kofun navbar}}

{{translated page|ja|牽牛子塚古墳|version=103767053|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[en:Kengoshizuka Kofun]]

<!--Kengoshizuka Kofun--><!--Kengoshizuka Kofun--><!--Kengoshizuka Kofun-->

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Kengoshizuka Kofun}}
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Kengoshizuka Kofun}}

{{wikidata link|Q11571102}}
[[Category:Empress Saimei]]
[[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]]
[[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]]
[[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]]
[[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]]
[[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]]
[[Category:Octagonal Kofun]]
[[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
